steps.title: Welcome to Pulsar
steps.description:
Welcome! This tutorial will show you Pulsar's two modes in action with your browser.

You will:
1. Press `Ctrl+Q` to open Switch Mode and switch to your browser
2. Press `Ctrl+Shift+Q` to open Command Mode and run a bookmarklet

You'll see how to switch to an app, then do something inside it.

steps.title: Switch Mode: app switching and launching
steps.description:
Press `Ctrl+Q` to open Switch Mode, hover over the browser icon (Chrome or Edge), then release the hotkey to switch to it.

That's all it takes — Pulsar jumps straight to your browser. No alt-tabbing, no searching.
steps.waitHintText: Press Ctrl+Q, hover over your browser icon, and release the hotkey.

steps.title: Switched to your browser
steps.description:
Great — you switched to your browser!

In the next step you'll use Command Mode to open a bookmarklet.

Click "Next" to continue.

steps.title: Command Mode: run an action
steps.description:
Press `Ctrl+Shift+Q` to open Command Mode, hover over "Open Bookmarklet", then release the hotkey.

Pulsar will open a webpage in your browser for you — that's Command Mode in action: running a plugin on the app you switched to.
steps.waitHintText: Press Ctrl+Shift+Q, hover over "Open Bookmarklet", and release the hotkey.

steps.title: Command executed!
steps.description:
Pulsar opened the bookmarklet in your browser! You just combined both modes:

1. Switch Mode (`Ctrl+Q`) — got you to your browser
2. Command Mode (`Ctrl+Shift+Q`) — opened a webpage inside it

That's the core Pulsar workflow.

steps.title: You're all set!
steps.description:
You just combined both Pulsar modes:

✔ Switched to your browser with Switch Mode (`Ctrl+Q`)
✔ Opened a bookmarklet with Command Mode (`Ctrl+Shift+Q`)

That's the core workflow: switch to any app, then act inside it.

Open Settings anytime to customize your slots, or keep using the defaults — Pulsar is ready whenever you are!
